movie.title: Michael
movie.overview: The story of Michael Jackson, one of the most influential artists the world has ever known, and his life beyond the music. His journey from the discovery of his extraordinary talent as the lead of the Jackson Five, to the visionary artist whose creative ambition fueled a relentless pursuit to become the biggest entertainer in the world, highlighting both his life off-stage and some of the most iconic performances from his early solo career.

alternativeTitles.title: 迈克尔·杰克逊：巨星之路

reviews.author: CinemaSerf
reviews.content: Given the whole slew of Jackson's involved in this stylish production, I couldn't help but feel a bit disappointed by the hollowness of the whole thing. Certainly Jaafar Jackson has something of his uncle's charm and childlike vulnerability, but were we not to have known he was a member of the actual family then I'm not sure I'd have been that convinced by his acting characterisation away from the dance floor. In any case, we begin with his ten year old (or is that eight?) younger years; with the domestic brutality at the hands of their domineering father Joe (Colman Domingo) whilst powerless mother Katherine (Nia Long) looked on - but without ever really getting to appreciate the nature of the bond between the siblings (and there's no sign of Janet). Despite being a couple of hours long, by the time we get to the adult, snakes and llamas, phase of his life we seem to be hurrying things along without really learning of his relationship with Berry Gordy (Larenz Tate) or Quincy Jones (Kendrick Simpson) or agent/manager/pillar of support John Branca (Miles Teller). There can be no doubting that this Jackson is in his element in the studio and on the stage; his performances border on the mimicry - but with a freshness to them that reminds us that this lad has a little bit of je ne sais quoi all of his own, something he adds charismatically throughout. The film looks every bit as glitzy and innovative as the original performances were but without any depth. Indeed, it comes across more as a sort of highly-polished fanzine that takes us to "Bad" with the promise of more to come, but without us really knowing a great deal more about him than we could have read on Wikipedia. There is something maniacal about Domingo's effort that convinces, but otherwise perhaps those making this were too close to the subject matter to be truly objective about what made the man tick, and so I left the cinema having remembered a man I saw at Wembley in 1988 fondly, but not especially enlightened.

reviews.author: Fernsjjf90
reviews.content: I super duper enjoyed the movie. It really brought back the Michael Jackson back from the grave. The fans of the whole wide world loved it. Antoine Fuqua did an excellent job not bringing the controversial of Michael Jackson. Just focusing uprising to the fame. I don't care what the critics say. Go and watch the movie yourself. Jaafar Jackson & Colman Domingo did excellent in the movie. my rating for the movie 9.5/10. You won't regret it from watching it all over again. Especially on IMAX format.

reviews.author: utsavlovesfilms
reviews.content:
_**A FABULOUS CINEMATIC EXPERIENCE**_

No matter how many times you watch it, the movie gets even better if you're a Michael Jackson fan. It offers a fast-paced look at the early life of the King of Pop without making it rushed. Basically tells how he became the King of Pop and what it took him to become that famous globally, including the abuse by his father and struggles with his lost childhood and appearance.

Despite enjoying Bohemian Rhapsody more in terms of its length and structure, Michael still offers a fresh look at his life, though a Bohemian Rhapsody structure would've made it even better, and most importantly, the runtime.

The film is only like 127 minutes while BoRhap clocked at 134 minutes, that was quite concerning because the film is not even 2 hours flat excluding the end credits, somehow that still felt better to me and that people with low attention span can enjoy it in theaters easily, especially the newer Tik-Tok generation.

Story: 8.2/10 (Well-written with humoristic content as well)
Pacing: 7.1/10 (Fast but not rushed, well done for newer gen citizens)
Structure: 5.3/10 (Too linear and less attractive at times)
Acting: 8/10 (Well-done, Colman, Juliano, Jaafar, Nia, and Miles nailed it)
Accuracy: 9.1/10 (Better timeline than Bohemian Rhapsody)
Exploration: 4.8/10 (Explores like a Wikipedia page and doesn't adds anything unique or unheard about Michael's life)
Drama: 8.9/10 (Well-done and executed perfectly)
Soundtrack: 7.3/10 (More of a greatest hits one by one but perfect picks)
CGI: 6.5/10 (Can feel distracting, especially with Bubbles, but doesn't ruins the experience)
Dancing: 9.8/10 (Literally a masterpiece, no flaws)
Cinematography: 7.5/10 (Feels crispy, though sometimes like specifically shot for newer gen)
Ending: 8.8/10 (Gives enough time to let audience prepare for Part Two, and ends on a high note)

Overall: 7.6/10 (⭐⭐⭐⭐)

reviews.author: Jun Superstar
reviews.content: 7/10. Entertaining but basic. Nothing new but it's great for beginners of Michael Jackson's catalog. Except there's a lot more info on the great Bubbles. If there is a Part 2, definitely more of Bubbles please.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: A biographical drama chronicling Michael Jackson's rise from Jackson Five prodigy to global solo superstar, exploring both his private life and landmark performances.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: Michael is a music-driven drama that traces the life of Michael Jackson beyond his recordings. It follows his discovery as the lead of the Jackson Five and his evolution into a visionary solo artist with relentless ambition. The film balances his off-stage personal life with recreations of some of his most iconic early solo performances. Ultimately, it portrays the making of one of the most influential entertainers in history.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: The film opens with young Michael Jackson's discovery of his extraordinary vocal and performance talent as the lead singer of the Jackson Five. Growing up in the spotlight, he quickly becomes the breakout star of the family group during their rise to Motown fame. As he matures, Michael's creative ambitions push him to launch a solo career that aims to make him the biggest entertainer in the world. The narrative explores his life off-stage, including the pressures of fame, his complex family dynamics, and his personal struggles behind closed doors. Viewers see his meticulous work ethic and visionary approach to music videos and live shows. The story highlights iconic early solo performances that defined his artistic identity and set new standards for pop spectacle. Relationships with mentors, collaborators, and the media shape his public and private personas. As his stardom skyrockets, the film examines the cost of his singular pursuit of greatness. It closes by reflecting on his lasting cultural impact as one of the most influential artists the world has ever known. Throughout, the drama interweaves musical set pieces with intimate character study.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: Michael is a dramatic music biopic that explores the life of Michael Jackson, focusing on his journey from a child prodigy fronting the Jackson Five to a trailblazing solo artist. The film delves into his off-stage life, revealing the personal experiences and relationships that shaped him away from the spotlight. It also showcases some of his most iconic early solo performances, illustrating how his creative vision transformed popular entertainment. Blending concert spectacle with intimate drama, the movie offers a portrait of the man behind the music and the ambition that drove his legendary career.

story_structure.climax: Reaches the peak of his early solo career with some of his most iconic and groundbreaking performances that cement his status as a global phenomenon.
story_structure.conflict: Michael faces the tension between his personal life off-stage and the immense pressure of his creative ambition to become the world's biggest entertainer.
story_structure.resolution: Provides a reflective look at his life beyond the music, acknowledging the duality of his public success and private world up to that point in his journey.
story_structure.introduction: Introduces Michael Jackson as a young boy whose extraordinary musical talent is discovered when he becomes the lead of the Jackson Five, setting the foundation for his future.
story_structure.risingAction: Chronicles his evolution from child star to visionary solo artist, detailing his relentless pursuit of artistic greatness and landmark early solo performances.

faq.answer: It tells the story of Michael Jackson, one of the most influential artists ever, covering his life beyond music, from his discovery as lead of the Jackson Five to his solo career and iconic performances.
faq.question: What is the movie Michael about?

faq.answer: No, Michael is a standalone biographical film and is not part of any franchise.
faq.question: Is this movie part of a franchise?

faq.answer: No, there is no post-credit scene in the movie Michael.
faq.question: Is there a post-credit scene?

faq.answer: The dominant genre is biographical drama with musical elements.
faq.question: What is the dominant genre?

faq.answer: The main subject is Michael Jackson, focusing on his life and career as a musician and entertainer.
faq.question: Who is the main subject of the film?

faq.answer: Yes, it highlights his discovery as the lead of the Jackson Five and his early career with the group.
faq.question: Does the film show Michael Jackson's early life with the Jackson Five?

faq.answer: Yes, the film highlights his life off-stage in addition to his musical achievements.
faq.question: Are Michael Jackson's off-stage life and struggles depicted?

faq.answer: Yes, it features some of the most iconic performances from Michael Jackson's early solo career.
faq.question: Does the movie include iconic performances from his solo career?

faq.answer: His creative ambition fueled a relentless pursuit to become the biggest entertainer in the world.
faq.question: What drove Michael Jackson's pursuit of fame according to the film?

faq.answer: Yes, it offers insight into one of the most influential artists in music history and his impact on entertainment.
